title: Hoffmannia regalis
intro: Hoffmannia regalis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Hoffmannia regalis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Hoffmannia regalis is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and pneumonia.
